$comment: SSOT — declarative registry of connectors. `providers` are the pluggable per-category backends (DNS/secrets/registry/observability): consumed by Go (go:embed, packages/core/categories) for module composition AND by the console codegen (apps/console/scripts/gen-connectors.mjs → lib/connectors/registry.generated.ts) for the Project selectors. `builtin` are the first-class cloud + git connectors (cloud federation / OAuth, handled outside the category module system): catalog rows only — seeded into the `connectors` table but NOT emitted to the TS registry. Both arrays are emitted into lib/db/seed/connectors.generated.sql. Go ignores `builtin`. Adding a pluggable provider = a `providers` row + a Go impl + an OpenTofu module under infra/templates/<module_path> — EXCEPT registry providers, whose only artifact is a dockerconfigjson imagePullSecret the runner seeds post-apply (no module; module_path is ""), because the in-tofu kubernetes provider is host+CA-only on AWS and can't create it.

builtin.name: Amazon Web Services
builtin.description: Provision EKS, networking, databases, and more in your AWS account via a cross-account IAM role.

builtin.name: Google Cloud
builtin.description: Provision GKE and managed services in your GCP project via Workload Identity Federation.

builtin.name: Microsoft Azure
builtin.description: Provision AKS and managed services in your Azure subscription via federated identity credentials.

builtin.name: Alibaba Cloud
builtin.description: Provision ACK and managed services on Alibaba Cloud via a RAM role (zero stored credentials).

builtin.name: DigitalOcean
builtin.description: Connect DigitalOcean with a scoped API token to provision DOKS and managed services. Provisioning templates coming soon.

builtin.name: Hetzner Cloud
builtin.description: Connect Hetzner Cloud with a scoped API token to provision a self-managed Talos Linux Kubernetes cluster on cheap VMs, with in-cluster data services via Helm.

builtin.name: Civo
builtin.description: Connect Civo with a scoped API token to provision managed K3s clusters. Provisioning templates coming soon.

builtin.name: GitHub
builtin.description: Connect repositories and wire GitOps deployments from GitHub.

builtin.name: GitLab
builtin.description: Connect repositories and wire GitOps deployments from GitLab.

builtin.name: Bitbucket
builtin.description: Connect repositories and wire GitOps deployments from Bitbucket.

providers.name: Cloudflare
providers.description: Manage DNS records and certificates on Cloudflare instead of the cluster cloud's native DNS.

fields.label: API Token
fields.help: A scoped Cloudflare API token with Zone:DNS:Edit permission.

fields.label: Zone ID
fields.help: The Cloudflare Zone ID for the domain.

providers.name: HashiCorp Vault
providers.description: Store and read application secrets from a HashiCorp Vault instance.

fields.label: Vault Address
fields.help: Base URL of the Vault server, e.g. https://vault.example.com:8200

providers.name: Docker Hub
providers.description: Use a Docker Hub repository as the container registry instead of the cloud-native one.

fields.label: Access Token
fields.help: A Docker Hub personal access token (Account Settings → Security).

fields.label: Namespace / Organization
fields.help: Defaults to the username if left blank.

providers.name: Generic Registry
providers.description: Authenticate to any OCI/Docker registry with a URL, username, and password.

fields.label: Password / Token
fields.help: The password or access token for the registry.

fields.label: Registry URL
fields.help: The registry endpoint, e.g. https://registry.example.com.

providers.name: GitHub Container Registry
providers.description: Pull images from GitHub Container Registry (ghcr.io).

fields.label: Username
fields.help: Your GitHub username.

fields.label: Personal Access Token
fields.help: A GitHub PAT with the read:packages scope.

providers.name: GitHub Enterprise Container Registry
providers.description: Pull images from a GitHub Enterprise Server container registry.

fields.label: Username
fields.help: Your GitHub Enterprise username.

fields.label: Personal Access Token
fields.help: A PAT with the read:packages scope on your GitHub Enterprise instance.

fields.label: Registry URL
fields.help: Your GitHub Enterprise container registry host, e.g. https://containers.github.example.com.

providers.name: GitLab Container Registry
providers.description: Pull images from the GitLab container registry.

fields.label: Username
fields.help: A GitLab username or deploy-token username.

fields.label: Token
fields.help: A deploy token or PAT with the read_registry scope.

fields.label: Registry URL
fields.help: Defaults to https://registry.gitlab.com; set for self-managed GitLab.

providers.name: Quay
providers.description: Pull images from Quay.io or a self-hosted Quay registry.

fields.label: Username
fields.help: A Quay username or robot account name.

fields.label: Token / Password
fields.help: The robot account token or account password.

fields.label: Registry URL
fields.help: Defaults to https://quay.io; set for a self-hosted Quay.

providers.name: Harbor
providers.description: Pull images from a self-hosted Harbor registry.

fields.label: Username
fields.help: A Harbor username or robot account name.

fields.label: Password / Secret
fields.help: The account password or robot account secret.

fields.label: Registry URL
fields.help: Your Harbor registry host, e.g. https://harbor.example.com.

providers.name: DigitalOcean Container Registry
providers.description: Pull images from the DigitalOcean container registry.

fields.label: API Token
fields.help: A DigitalOcean API token with registry read access. Used as both username and password.

providers.name: Scaleway Container Registry
providers.description: Pull images from the Scaleway container registry.

fields.label: Secret Key
fields.help: A Scaleway secret key. The registry username is fixed to 'nologin'.

fields.label: Registry URL
fields.help: Your Scaleway registry endpoint, e.g. https://rg.fr-par.scw.cloud.

providers.name: Amazon ECR (cross-account)
providers.description: Pull from an ECR registry in a different AWS account — keyless, no stored key. The cluster mints a short-lived pull token in-cluster from a role the target account trusts.

fields.label: Target AWS Account ID
fields.help: The AWS account that owns the ECR registry (the account the cluster pulls from cross-account).

fields.label: Region
fields.help: The ECR registry region, e.g. us-east-1.

fields.label: Registry Host
fields.help: The registry endpoint, e.g. 123456789012.dkr.ecr.us-east-1.amazonaws.com.

fields.label: Target Role ARN
fields.help: An IAM role in the target account that trusts this cluster's workload identity and grants ECR pull (GetAuthorizationToken + BatchGetImage). You create this in the registry account.

providers.name: Google Artifact Registry (cross-project)
providers.description: Pull from an Artifact Registry repository in a different GCP project — keyless, no stored key. The cluster mints a short-lived token in-cluster from a service account the target project granted reader.

fields.label: Target GCP Project ID
fields.help: The GCP project that owns the Artifact Registry repository.

fields.label: Region
fields.help: The Artifact Registry location, e.g. us-central1.

fields.label: Registry Host
fields.help: The registry endpoint, e.g. us-central1-docker.pkg.dev.

fields.label: Reader Service Account
fields.help: The service account the target project granted roles/artifactregistry.reader on the repository. You grant this in the registry project.

providers.name: Azure Container Registry (cross-subscription)
providers.description: Pull from an ACR in a different Azure subscription — keyless, no stored key. The cluster mints a short-lived token in-cluster from an identity the target ACR granted AcrPull.

fields.label: Target Subscription ID
fields.help: The Azure subscription that owns the ACR.

fields.label: Region
fields.help: The ACR region (optional).

fields.label: Registry Host
fields.help: The registry endpoint, e.g. myregistry.azurecr.io.

fields.label: Pull Identity Client ID
fields.help: The client id of the identity the target ACR granted AcrPull. You grant this in the registry subscription.

providers.name: Datadog
providers.description: Ship cluster metrics, logs, and traces to Datadog.

providers.name: Grafana Cloud
providers.description: Remote-write metrics to Grafana Cloud's managed Prometheus.

fields.label: Remote Write URL
fields.help: The Grafana Cloud Prometheus remote_write endpoint.

providers.name: HTTPS Helm Repository
providers.description: Authenticate to a classic index.yaml Helm chart repository (https://charts.example.com) with HTTP basic auth.

fields.label: Password / Token
fields.help: The password or access token for the chart repository.

fields.label: Repository URL
fields.help: The chart repository URL, e.g. https://charts.example.com.

providers.name: Docker Hub (OCI Helm)
providers.description: Pull Helm charts from Docker Hub's OCI registry (registry-1.docker.io).

fields.label: Access Token
fields.help: A Docker Hub access token.

providers.name: GitHub Container Registry (OCI Helm)
providers.description: Pull Helm charts from GitHub Container Registry (ghcr.io).

fields.label: Username
fields.help: Your GitHub username.

fields.label: Password / Token
fields.help: A GitHub PAT with the read:packages scope.

providers.name: GitLab Container Registry (OCI Helm)
providers.description: Pull Helm charts from a GitLab container registry (registry.gitlab.com or a self-managed host).

fields.label: Password / Token
fields.help: A GitLab deploy or personal access token.

fields.label: Registry Host
fields.help: The registry host (optional; defaults to registry.gitlab.com).

providers.name: Generic OCI Registry (Helm)
providers.description: Pull Helm charts from any OCI-compliant registry (Harbor, JFrog, a self-hosted distribution) with a host, username, and password.

fields.label: Password / Token
fields.help: The password or access token for the registry.

fields.label: Registry Host
fields.help: The OCI registry host, e.g. registry.example.com.

providers.name: Scaleway Container Registry (OCI Helm)
providers.description: Pull Helm charts from the Scaleway container registry. The username is fixed to 'nologin'.

fields.label: Secret Key
fields.help: A Scaleway secret key. The registry username is fixed to 'nologin'.

fields.label: Registry Host
fields.help: Your Scaleway registry host, e.g. rg.fr-par.scw.cloud.

providers.name: Amazon ECR (OCI Helm)
providers.description: Pull Helm charts from Amazon ECR. Coming soon: ECR uses a 12-hour ephemeral token, so its keyless in-cluster token refresh (from the connected AWS account) is a documented follow-up.

fields.label: Registry Host
fields.help: The ECR registry host, e.g. 1234.dkr.ecr.us-east-1.amazonaws.com.

fields.label: Region
fields.help: The ECR region.

providers.name: Amazon ECR Public (OCI Helm)
providers.description: Pull Helm charts from Amazon ECR Public (public.ecr.aws). Coming soon: keyless in-cluster token refresh is a documented follow-up.

fields.label: Registry Host
fields.help: The ECR Public registry host (public.ecr.aws).
